properties.title: Properties
properties.description: {selectedCount} selected elements{{layerCount, plural, =0 {} other { | {layerCount} DXF layers}}

deleteConfirm.title: Delete floorplan
deleteConfirm.description: The DXF floorplan and its saved colored layers will be deleted. This action cannot be undone.

duplicateFloorplan.title: Copy floorplan to another floor
duplicateFloorplan.description: Copy the DXF floorplan of floor "{source}" to another floor. The destination floor will be re-drawn from the original DXF.
duplicateFloorplan.noSourceFloorplan: The source floor has no saved DXF floorplan.
duplicateFloorplan.noDestinations: No other floor available as destination.
duplicateFloorplan.wipeWarning: The destination floor already contains {polygons} polygons and will be replaced.

importedMeshes.title: Imported
importedMeshes.empty: No imported meshes on this floor.

delete.title: Delete profile
delete.description: The profile "{name}" will be permanently deleted.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete style
deleteConfirm.description: The style "{name}" will be permanently deleted.

createDialog.title: New Dimension Style
createDialog.errorDuplicate: A style with this name already exists.

textOverride.fieldHint: Type < to insert a field token

inlineTab.title: Line Properties
inlineTab.emptyState: Select a line to view and edit its pattern segments.
inlineTab.hint: Add segments (Dash / Gap / Dot) to define a pattern for this line.
inlineTab.editHint: Editing updates every line of this type.
inlineTab.compoundHint: Complex line type (multi-layer or with symbols/text) — edit it from the line-type dialog.

linePatternEditor.title: New line type
linePatternEditor.description: Define explicit lengths for dashes, gaps and dots. The pattern is saved and selectable by name on any dimension.

name.taken: A line type with this name already exists.

pattern.needsVisible: Needs at least one dash or dot.
pattern.needsGap: Needs at least one gap in between.
pattern.badLength: Lengths must be greater than zero.
pattern.textEmpty: Enter text for the text segment.

empty.supportedFormats: Supports: PDF, PNG, JPG, WEBP, TIFF

calibration.title: 2-Point Calibration
calibration.remapDesc: {count} polygons will be remapped to the new scale.
calibration.instructionA: Click the first point on the background
calibration.instructionB: Click the second point on the background

replace.title: Replace background
replace.description: The existing background will be replaced with "{fileName}". Continue?

layerActions.selectAllEntities: Click to select all entities, Ctrl+Click for multi-selection
layerActions.layer0Tooltip: System layer — rename and delete not allowed

layerValidation.RESERVED: Layer "0" is reserved by the system
layerValidation.DUPLICATE: A layer with this name already exists

sceneInfo.title: Scene Information
sceneInfo.importHint: Import a DXF file to view information

overlayProperties.title: Overlay Properties
overlayProperties.selectOverlay: Select an overlay to view its properties.
overlayProperties.label: Label
overlayProperties.transferDescription: This entity is already linked to another polygon. Transfer the link to the current polygon?
overlayProperties.statusLinkedHint: Status is determined by the linked entity
overlayProperties.noFloorLinked: Link this level to a floor first (Level Panel)

overlayList.title: Overlays
overlayList.deleteConfirmDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this overlay? This action cannot be undone.

pdfPanel.title: PDF Background
pdfPanel.selectFile: Select a PDF file to use as background

statusBar.distMeasureDesc: Ephemeral distance/path measurement — not saved. Enter finishes, ESC clears.
statusBar.snapDesc: Grip step — hold Q while dragging (else free)
statusBar.dimHudDesc: Show length & angle readouts on all entities (drawing & grip editing, 2D & 3D). Does not affect area/perimeter or thickness/height.
statusBar.dirArcDesc: Coloured rotation-direction arc while drawing a line (green/red)
statusBar.listeningDimDesc: Show cyan distances to neighbouring elements on the line ghost
statusBar.lineweightDesc: Show lineweights — fixed on screen regardless of zoom

labels.createNewOrChangeFilters: Create a new layer or change the filters

readOnly.title: Layers
readOnly.waitForAdmins: Wait for layers to be created by administrators

stepDescription.company: Select the company that manages the project
stepDescription.project: Select the project the floor plan belongs to
stepDescription.building: Select the building of the floor plan
stepDescription.floor: Select the floor of the floor plan
stepDescription.destination: Select the final destination for storage

destinationSelector.noCompanies: No companies found in the system.
destinationSelector.noProjects: No projects found for the selected company.
destinationSelector.noBuildings: No buildings found for the selected project.
destinationSelector.noFloors: No floors found for the selected building.

tooltip.viewScaleHint: Actual on-screen scale (depends on zoom) — not the drawing/annotation scale

aria.viewScale: View scale {scale}. Click to fit, double-click for actual size, right-click for menu.

topography.title: Topography — Contours
topography.subtitle: Load survey points (X Y Z) and generate contour lines.
topography.loadLabel: Points file (X Y Z per line)

contourStyle.title: Contours: exact ↔ smooth
contourStyle.hint: Same contours, two looks: exact (they sit on the surveyed points) or smooth (a gentle curve for presentation).
contourStyle.exportNote: Legal export (Cadastre) always uses the exact contours.

pointLabels.title: Point labels
pointLabels.hint: Selective label per point type: elevation on every point, X,Y only on boundary vertices — never on all points.
pointLabels.boundaryHint: No boundary set — pick a site boundary for vertex X,Y.

status.generated: {count, plural, one {# label created} other {# labels created}}.

error.nothing-selected: Select at least one label type.

grid.title: ΕΓΣΑ87 coordinate grid
grid.hint: Geodetic coordinate grid: crosses at round ΕΓΣΑ87 values + perimeter Easting/Northing numbering. Separate from the drawing-aid GRID (F7).

status.baked: {count, plural, one {# grid entity created} other {# grid entities created}}.

error.no-layers: Could not create the grid layer.
error.empty-grid: Step too large for the extent — no grid produced.

north.title: North arrow
north.hint: Shows North on the drawing. «True» = with ΕΓΣΑ87 meridian-convergence correction; «Grid» = parallel to the coordinate grid lines.

status.baked: {count, plural, one {# arrow entity created} other {# arrow entities created}}.
status.exists: The north arrow already exists in the drawing.

error.no-layers: Could not create the north-arrow layer.

status.loaded: {count, plural, one {# point loaded} other {# points loaded}}
status.skipped: {count, plural, one {# line skipped} other {# lines skipped}}

breakline.title: Breaklines
breakline.hint: Mark existing lines (road edge, ridge, ditch) so the surface keeps the hard break instead of smoothing across it.
breakline.count: {count, plural, =0 {No breaklines} one {# breakline} other {# breaklines}}

geoRef.title: Geo-referencing (snap onto terrain)
geoRef.hint: Snap the plan onto the survey (real ΕΓΣΑ'87 coordinates) so the building sits on the ground on every floor.
geoRef.autoAlignHint: Quick estimate: matches the building center to the terrain center. Refine it with a common point.
geoRef.scaleWarn: Warning: the two points are not the same distance apart (ratio {ratio}). Check units.

status.autoNoData: Both plan and survey points are required.
status.needPoint: Pick a point and enter its ΕΓΣΑ coordinate.

terrain3d.title: Terrain in 3D
terrain3d.hint: The same surface the contours are cut from, as solid relief in the 3D view.

cloud3d.title: Point cloud in 3D
cloud3d.hint: The last imported cloud, over the terrain: brown = ground, grey = what the filter discarded. It is a view only — never measured, never snapped to.

import.title: Import survey points
import.sourceHint: Pick a point file (CSV, TXT, XYZ), an Excel sheet, a DXF drawing with points/elevations, or a point cloud (LAS, LAZ) from a drone/LiDAR survey.
import.confirmHint: These points will replace the ones currently loaded.
import.confirmCount: {count, plural, one {# point ready to import} other {# points ready to import}}
import.previewSkipped: {count, plural, one {# row will be skipped} other {# rows will be skipped}}

preset.PNEZD: PNEZD — No., Northing(Y), Easting(X), Elev., Description
preset.PENZD: PENZD — No., Easting(X), Northing(Y), Elev., Description

pointcloud.forceCsf: Re-run CSF classification even if the file already carries one

columns.title: File columns
columns.hint: Proposed from the file's own data — check it before filtering. A wrong column raises no error: it produces a plausible-looking cloud of the wrong site.
columns.incomplete: Without a complete X/Y/Z assignment the reader takes the first three numeric fields of each line.

csf.title: Bare-earth filter (CSF)
csf.hint: Applied only when the file carries no classification already, or when forced below.

unit.title: File units
unit.hint: A LAS/LAZ file does not state its unit in the header. Pick the unit that gives a sensible site extent — a wrong unit silently reads the cloud at the wrong scale.

result.sourceClassified: The file already carried a classification — it was honoured as-is.

error.notLas: Not a valid LAS file — check whether this is an AutoCAD Layer State (.las) file instead.
error.lasTruncated: The LAS file is incomplete or corrupted.
error.lazUnsupported: The file is compressed LAZ and cannot be read as uncompressed LAS.
error.lazRuntimeUnavailable: LAZ decompression could not be loaded in this browser.
error.lazDecodeFailed: LAZ decompression failed — the file may be corrupt.
error.unsupportedPdrf: Unsupported LAS point data record format.
error.clothTooFine: The cloth resolution is too fine for the cloud's extent — increase it.
error.invalidClothResolution: Invalid cloth resolution — it must be a positive number.
error.invalidCellSize: Invalid point spacing — it must be a positive number.
error.unexpected: Unexpected error while processing the point cloud.

warn.strideSampled: The file was too large — a sampled subset of points was kept.
warn.skippedLines: Some lines in the file were skipped (invalid coordinates).
warn.truncatedPointData: The LAS point data looked incomplete — what was available was read.
warn.recordLengthMismatch: The LAS record length differs from what its format expects.
warn.headerBoundsInvalid: The LAS header bounds were invalid — recomputed from the points themselves.
warn.spanImplausible: The cloud's extent looks unrealistic — check the file unit (feet or millimetres read as metres?) and the columns.

cutfill.title: Earthworks volumes (cut / fill)
cutfill.hint: How much soil must be dug out or filled in — against a design level or a designed surface.
cutfill.proposedLoaded: {count, plural, one {# designed-surface point} other {# designed-surface points}}
cutfill.boundaryNone: No boundary — computed over the whole survey.
cutfill.skipped: {count, plural, one {# triangle skipped} other {# triangles skipped}}
cutfill.crossCheckWarn: Warning — the two methods diverge by {value}%.
cutfill.analysisHint: Show the 3D terrain to see where cut and fill occur.

error.no-surface: No ground surface — load survey points first.
error.no-proposed-surface: No designed surface loaded to compare against.

qa.title: Quality check (alarm bell)
qa.hint: Automatic checks on the data — suspect elevations, duplicate points, boundaries, breaklines. You decide.
qa.notEnoughData: Fewer than 3 points — no surface to check.
qa.allClear: No findings — the survey passed the checks.
qa.dropped: {count, plural, one {# more finding not shown (report cap)} other {# more findings not shown (report cap)}}

flag.elevationBust: Suspect node elevation: Z={elevation}m while neighbours ~{expected}m (deviation {deviation}m).
flag.duplicatePoint: Duplicate points #{a} and #{b} at the same spot with incompatible elevation (Δ {deviation}m).
flag.selfIntersectionBoundary: The boundary crosses itself — invalid loop.
flag.degenerateBoundary: The boundary is degenerate (area {area}m²) — likely a pick mistake.
flag.missingBreakline: Steep {angle}° fold with no constraint — a ridge/ditch breakline may be missing.

autoBreakline.title: Automatic breaklines
autoBreakline.hint: The system reads the surface and finds where the ground breaks (road edges, ditches, retaining walls). It PROPOSES them — none is added without your approval.
autoBreakline.added: {count, plural, one {# breakline added} other {# breaklines added}}
autoBreakline.found: {count, plural, one {# candidate — shown green on the drawing} other {# candidates — shown green on the drawing}}
autoBreakline.none: No candidates — the surface breaks nowhere sharply without a constraint.
autoBreakline.notEnoughData: No surface — at least 3 points are required.
autoBreakline.candidate: {length} m · fold {angle}° · {edges, plural, one {# edge} other {# edges}}
autoBreakline.dropped: {count, plural, one {# more candidate not shown (report cap)} other {# more candidates not shown (report cap)}}

deliverables.title: Export deliverables folder
deliverables.hint: One button for the Greek survey deliverable: tables, diagram and tolerance check.
deliverables.done: Folder “{file}” created ({tables, plural, one {# table} other {# tables}} in the drawing).
deliverables.coordinatesFileOnly: The coordinate table was too large for the drawing — kept in the files only.

warn.no-points: No points — coordinate table was not produced.
warn.no-boundary: No boundary set — no area measurement or tolerance check.
warn.no-volumes: No volumes computed — earthworks table was not included.

verdict.not-declared: Tolerance check: no title values declared.
